content.question: Does the Finnish quasi-experimental finding of closures accelerating (rather than following) community decline represent a causal mechanism specific to rural Nordic contexts, or would it generalize to Hungarian village economies with different labor-market, agricultural, and family-structure conditions?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a parallel quasi-experimental study in Hungarian villages (DID with matching on pre-closure demographics) comparing population trends 2010–2025 in matched pairs of villages with/without primary school closures, controlling for commuting-shed distance to urban employment centers and secondary-school availability.
